summary: Multi-PR squash merge with no body or review text. The diff replaces several `/// #` stubs with C across Kirby copy ability setup/dispatch, common fighter dead/rebirth logic, game/menu/library/toy helpers, and finishes `sysdolphin/baselib/spline.c` as matching. It also updates exact struct layouts, prototypes, includes, symbol metadata, and build config needed for matching.
what_changed: Changed `configure.py` so `sysdolphin/baselib/spline.c` is built as `Object(Matching, ...)` instead of `Object(NonMatching, ...)`.

Adjusted `spline.c`/`spline.h` for matching by changing helper linkage/inlining, adding `spl_GetArcLengthDx`, removing the exported `splGetBSplinePoint` declaration, and updating sdata2 symbols from `HSD_Spline_804DE620`-style names to numbered `@13`/`@14`/etc labels.

Decompiled Kirby SpecialN functions `ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800F19AC`, and `ftKb_SpecialN_800F1A8C`; the dispatch functions switch on `fp->fv.kb.hat.kind` and call copied-fighter-specific helpers.
Expanded `ftCommon_MotionVars.unk_deadup` with `x4C`, `x50`, and `x5C` fields, enabling `ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys` to use typed float/Vec3 motion data.

Decompiled `ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys` and `ftCo_800D5600`, including fall/rebirth motion handling, vector math, collision refresh, motion-state transition to `ftCo_MS_RebirthWait`, and accessory callback selection.
Decompiled `gm_80164504`, which maps a stage ID through `Stage_8022519C`, looks up unlock metadata in `lbl_803B790C`, notifies via `gmMainLib_8015D818`, and sets the stage unlock bit.
Decompiled library helpers `lbArq_80014D2C`, `fn_800244F4`, and `fn_8001EB14` for ARQ list initialization, audio-global reset, and THP decoder file/header initialization.
Expanded `THPDecComp` with concrete header/state fields and added `THPInit`, `DVDConvertPathToEntrynum`, and `lbFile_800161C4`-driven setup in `lbmthp`.
Decompiled menu/name functions `fn_8024FC48`, `mn_802307F8`, and `mnNameNew_8023B224`, adding `mn_802307F8_t` and concrete prototypes for text/menu transitions.
Decompiled `un_80307828` in `toy.c`, extending `TyCameraData_` and resetting camera/light state before calling `HSD_CObjSetInterest`.
smart_moves: The spline match was finished with minimal codegen-focused changes: helper functions were made `static`/`inline`, a tiny arithmetic helper isolated `(midpoint - start) / 8.0F`, and public headers/symbols were adjusted to match actual linkage.
The PR updated struct layouts at the same time as decompiling users of those fields, avoiding raw offset soup in later code (`unk_deadup`, `THPDecComp`, `TyCameraData_`, and `mn_802307F8_t`).
Kirby copy-ability dispatch reuses existing per-character SpecialN helpers and preserves grouped cases like Fox/Falco and Link/Young Link rather than duplicating behavior.
Concrete prototypes replaced `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` in touched headers, making future callsites safer and more searchable.
Matching-specific artifacts were kept local and explicit, including `#pragma dont_inline`, `PAD_STACK`, exact casts, `goto` labels, and a `1LL` bit shift where the diff suggests assembly needed them.
decomp_lessons: When a stub depends on motion variables or file headers, first recover the exact struct offsets and then express the function in typed fields; this produced cleaner matches for `ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys` and `fn_8001EB14`.
For baselib matching, exported-vs-static helper status matters. If assembly does not export a helper such as `splGetBSplinePoint`, remove it from the public header and give it internal linkage.
Global reset/init functions often match by preserving exact assignment order and declaring every touched static/extern global, as shown by `fn_800244F4`.
Menu text functions commonly release an existing `HSD_Text*`, allocate a new SIS text object, set `font_size`, and emit a byte string ID; reuse that pattern for nearby menu decompilation.
`PAD_STACK` and compiler pragmas are acceptable matching tactics in this codebase, but the evidence here supports using them narrowly around the exact function that needs them.
This PR has no review discussion or body, so future rationale should be checked in source PRs #2366, #2367, and #2368 if more context is needed.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions keep subsystem/address names such as `fn_800244F4`, `fn_8001EB14`, `fn_8024FC48`, and `un_80307828` until semantic names are justified.
Recovered anonymous struct fields use offset-style names and padding, e.g. `x4C`, `x50`, `x5C`, `pad114`, `unk_128`, and `x58`, with comments documenting offsets.
Kirby copied-ability functions follow existing prefixes like `ftKb_SpecialN*` plus character abbreviations such as `Ic`, `Pe`, `Fx`, `Lk`, `Mt`, `Ns`, `Ss`, `Sk`, `Ys`, `Pr`, and `Gw`.
Header declarations were upgraded from generic `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete return/argument types when the function body made them clear.
Spline constants in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` were no longer kept as descriptive `HSD_Spline_804DE...` globals; the matching version uses compiler-style numbered `@` labels.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `sysdolphin/baselib/spline.c` was moved to matching by tuning `static`/`inline` linkage, declaration order, midpoint/result temporaries, and a small `spl_GetArcLengthDx` helper.
`ftKb_SpecialN_800F10D4` uses `#pragma dont_inline on`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, a stack buffer `sp14[0x90]`, and the unusual `Fighter* fp = fp = gobj->user_data` pattern.
`ftCo_DeadUpFall_Phys` casts `p_ftCommonData + 0x520` as a byte base and then accesses float/Vec3 values via explicit offsets to reproduce the original common-data access pattern.
`gm_80164504` preserves branch shape with `goto found_stage` and `goto found_notify`, explicit `(s32)` casts in comparisons, and `1LL << unlock_idx` for the bitmask update.
`lbArq_80014D2C` ends with `PAD_STACK(8)`, and `un_80307828` ends with `PAD_STACK(12)`, indicating stack-shape padding was part of the match.
The spline symbol-map update keeps the sdata2 addresses but changes symbol names, showing that constant-label naming can affect or reflect final matching state.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice.
The PR body is null; intent is inferred from the title, changed files, diff, and auto-merge metadata rather than reviewer discussion.
Because this is titled as a merge of #2366, #2367, and #2368, inspect those individual PRs for any missing review rationale.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #2366, PR #2367, and PR #2368 individually for review comments or rationale absent from this merge PR.
Audit `gm_80164504` return type: the diff declares `int` but the shown body has no explicit return.
Rename or document recovered fields in `THPDecComp`, `unk_deadup`, `TyCameraData_`, and audio globals once neighboring code gives stronger semantic evidence.
Verify the intended `lbArqGlobal`/`lbArqNode` free-list node count and structure layout around `lbArq_80014D2C`.
After more spline work, confirm whether the numbered sdata2 `@` labels should remain or be given stable local names without breaking matching.
